channel.description: Recent content in plugin on BrunoSXS

item.title: Using SMRT-Godot - The dialog system with a fancy name
item.description: This post is long due, but I found some motivation after talking to Nathan(from GDQuest, featured in my list of gamedev resources). In here I’ll show a simple project to highlight some of the features of SMRT. You can find this project in SMRT’s examples folder at the project’s github repository, download it and follow along. Play the scene and interact with the characters before proceeding… Done? Let’s just go over the basic concept of SMRT: chapters, dialogs and texts

item.title: Update to SMRT-Godot
item.description: Just updated SMRT-Godot with some bugfixes and changes after some months compiling feedback. What is SMRT-Godot? It is a dialog system for your games, written as a plugin for godot that enables you to create classic dialog boxes with a lot of personalization. See it in action: I have just sent this new version to godot’s assetlib, so it is probably not updated there, but on the repo you can find the latest version and some examples.
